page: Amazon Web Services m8gn.48xlarge is a general-purpose, network and EBS-optimized server instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features 192 dedicated physical cores using the arm64 architecture, specifically the AWS Neoverse-V2 ARMv9 processor running at 2.7 GHz. The system is equipped with 768.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. There is no local storage or GPU hardware bundled with this instance type.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier multi-core and memory performance. The instance achieves a top-tier stress-ng multi-core score of 313,816.03 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 20,454.0. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, with read-only small block cached bandwidth reaching 21,326.99 GB/sec and uncached large block write bandwidth at 483.72 GB/sec. Database and caching benchmarks are also in the top tier, with Redis SET operations reaching 357.24 million operations per second and PassMark database operations scoring 46,979.23. Single-core performance is moderate, with a stress-ng single-core score of 1,643.7 and a Geekbench single-core score of 1,655.0.

The instance provides a baseline network bandwidth of 300 Gbps, making it highly optimized for network-heavy and EBS-reliant workloads. Qualitatively, the ARM-based architecture offers high resource density and exceptional multi-threaded performance efficiency for large-scale deployments, though the lack of local storage requires external network-attached storage. This server is highly suited for multi-threaded applications, high-throughput database operations, static web serving, software compilation, and large-scale general-purpose enterprise workloads.
description: Amazon Web Services m8gn.48xlarge is a general-purpose, network and EBS-optimized instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor and 192 dedicated ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 physical cores running at 2.7 GHz. It features 768.0 GB of RAM, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB per core ratio, with no local storage or GPU hardware. The instance delivers top-tier multi-core and memory performance, as evidenced by a stress-ng multi-core score of 313,816.03 and memory bandwidth of up to 21,326.99 GB/sec for cached reads. It also excels in high-throughput scenarios, achieving 357.24 million Redis SET operations per second and 155.49 GB/sec in static web serving. With a baseline network bandwidth of 300 Gbps, this instance is highly suited for large-scale multi-threaded applications, database operations, software compilation, and high-throughput web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8gn.48xlarge is an arm64 general-purpose instance with 192 dedicated Neoverse-V2 cores, 768 GB RAM, and 300 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier multi-core and database performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m8gn.48xlarge is an arm64 instance featuring 192 dedicated cores, 768 GB RAM, and 300 Gbps network bandwidth for high-throughput workloads.
tagline: A high-core ARM-based instance delivering exceptional multi-threaded performance, massive memory bandwidth, and 300 Gbps baseline network throughput.
bullet_points: Powered by 192 dedicated AWS Neoverse-V2 ARMv9 cores at 2.7 GHz
Equipped with 768.0 GB of system memory (4.0 GB per core)
Provides a high-speed baseline network bandwidth of 300 Gbps
Top-tier multi-core performance and memory bandwidth in benchmarks
Optimized for high-throughput database operations and static web serving
No local storage or GPU hardware included
